message:
Welcome to Once Upon a Time in Production, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

*Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
*Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey Everyone just in, customers are reporting the site images are not appearing, is this true?.
Hey Bob let me check
Sure, let me know what you find—customers are saying product images aren't showing up and some pages look broken.
/help
Let me know if you need any details on customer impact or support tickets—reports are still coming in about missing images.
I get a server error
:star: Reproduces the incident
I'm also seeing some images fail to load with errors, like the mug and sunglasses.
I can see all, let me click on something
When I click around, some images like the mug and sunglasses aren't loading for me either.
Ah yes some images are not there. Was there a deployment?
Checking recent changes...
:star: Check changes done to the platform
there were some product catalog UAT deployments
I did deploy a chaos test for ProductCatalogueService earlier.
But that's UAT only — nothing in prod.
Also added some extra logging in checkout.
Just log fields, shouldn't affect performance.
I pushed a small frontend UI tweak earlier.
Loading animation + accessibility stuff.
No backend or infra changes.
Confirmed: the chaos test and other ProductCatalogueService changes are only live in UAT, not production.
lets rollback those to start with, just in case. I am raising a sev2
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Understood, I'll start rolling back the recent ProductCatalogueService changes in UAT now.
Please note, these changes were not deployed to production, so this rollback shouldn't affect the live site.
*Incident Number INC1071:* SEV2 some images not returning
Just to confirm, there have been no recent changes to the image delivery pipeline or related infrastructure on the platform side.
Which regions are affected by this image issue?
Looks like all of them but let me check
Bob is everyone affected by this issue?
checking reports, let me get back to you on what customers are saying
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Hamed do we have a special repository for these images
From what we can tell, the site isn't fully down. Customers can load pages and navigate categories, but something feels off visually
A few people mentioned pages look “incomplete” or “not finished loading”
Yes I tried and can click on the faulty image and still managed to progress
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
Hamed is currently out of office but should be available very shortly; I can check our documentation in the meantime.
double checking reports....
I need to know how many customers or orders are impacted—give me numbers.
thanks. Can I also get Maya and Tanya to review any possible issue
Getting over 100 reports so far on this
Looks like the common thread is that product images are not loading, and customers are seeing broken image icons instead. This is consistent across browsers
from everywhere? any specific location?
Definitely a global issue
I'll review the CDN and image storage logs for any anomalies.
I'll double-check the ProductCatalogueService endpoints and error rates for anything unusual.
thank you
Reports are coming in from all regions, so this appears to be a global issue rather than regional.
Let us know if you need more updates or a summary for leadership.
OK let me update BEz
Thanks, please keep us posted if BEz has any additional questions or requests.
*This is a global issue so far, I'll try and quantify for you but sales are donw although you can still click on the image that has not loaded and progress to payment*
:star: Qualify the problem
How much revenue have we lost so far, and what is the current rate of lost sales per hour?
Can we quantify the issue? Do we know how many sales we've lost?
We have over 100 customer reports so far, but we don't have exact sales loss numbers yet.
Tanya and Maya any news?
:star: Manage actions and timings
No new errors in ProductCatalogueService logs, but I'm still reviewing the last deployment diffs.
CDN logs show a spike in 404 errors for product images starting about 45 minutes ago.
OK Who provides us with the images?
The images are provided by our merchandising team and uploaded to our central image storage, which is then distributed via CDN.
We should look deeper into Product catalogue service - this may not be due to change
There are spikes in traffic
OK lets do that
I'll coordinate updates and make sure everyone is aligned on next steps.
Bob are you able to quantify the loss for now?
We can't quantify the exact sales loss yet, but we know over 100 customers have reported issues and product pages are not displaying correctly.
Security team should look deeper into what may be he cause of spike

so fare we are:
1 - looking at prod catalogue service
2 - Looking into CDN issues
3 - quantifying losses
That's correct, and we're also monitoring customer reports for any escalation in volume or severity.
we are not able to quantify losses yet but over 100 custoemrs have reported
I need a precise estimate of affected orders and revenue impact as soon as possible.
tinus can you help us with financial implication of this incident
I'll pull transaction logs and estimate the potential revenue impact based on drop-off rates and failed checkouts during the incident window.
looking into this if is possible at this early stages
thanks, Bez wants to know
I'll prioritize the financial analysis and share a summary with you and BEz as soon as possible.
thanks
Let us know if you need a consolidated incident report or further updates for leadership.
Tinus is on the case
Maya and Tanya? Any news?
Thanks
No new errors found in the last deployment, but I'm still checking for any subtle issues in the product catalogue integration.
can we also contact the team that provides us with the images?
Keep me updated on progress and provide numbers as soon as you have them.
I'll reach out to the merchandising team immediately and ask them to confirm if there were any recent changes or issues with image uploads.
thanks
You're welcome, and I'll keep everyone updated as we learn more.
Anything else we can check?
@PLAYER can we please tag maya to look into CDN related high traffic issue on Product catalogue service
We could also check if there were any recent changes to CDN configuration or permissions that might have affected image delivery.
OK Thanks Maya also "ook into CDN related high traffic issue on Product catalogue service" as requested by Daniel
Checking infra now, give me a moment.
We see a very high spike in requests coming to Product catalogue service
Seeing a lot more traffic reaching backend services than usual.
were there any deployments for this service?
PCS request rate is way above baseline.
Been doing a comparison with Checkout and Frontend.
And they seem fine, let me show you
https://i.ibb.co/fVY8zWQj/Checkout.png
https://i.ibb.co/Kk5gHqt/Frontend.png
https://i.ibb.co/chz2PJSH/PCS.png
daniel the chaos test, can you pls check it again?
PCS is the clear outlier.
Might be worth looping in Security to rule out something external.
There was a deployment to ProductCatalogueService yesterday, but it only included a chaos test for latency in UAT and should not affect production.
The chaos test is only enabled in UAT, not in production, but I'll double-check the deployment config and feature flags to be sure.
thanks
I'll report back as soon as I've confirmed the chaos test is fully disabled in production.
maya any external influence?
Looking at the alerts now.
Seeing a big spike hitting image-related endpoints.
WAF rate-limit rules are firing.
ALERT: image-endpoint-rate-limit threshold=4,000/5min observed=15,000 action=BLOCK
ALERT: anomalous-request-rate endpoint=/productCatalogue status=triggered
Are we ddos'ing ourselves?
:star: Form a working theory
I'm calling this for now — looks like we're under a DDoS attack.
Before we start mitigation please update the business
Checking if the traffic is legit...
So the IPs don't show typical attack patterns
What I see is legitimate user traffic behaving badly probably because something upstream isn't working as expected. The WAF is reacting to the volume, not intent.
Bez I have been told we may be under attack, DDOS to be precise, we're looking into it
so we're doing this to ourself,
The traffic seems legit
Im now confident that this is not a DDOS attack
May be an internal thing
Sorry for the delay, i just got in can you fill me in on whats going on?
If this is a DDOS, what is the projected timeline for mitigation and when will normal service resume?
What is causing the PCS to struggle, what is it getting requests for?
We have a high volume traffic hitting image related endpoints
Wait images are served from S3 through CloudFront. PCS was the old path for images
Oh thats right i forgot PCS just returns the image URLs.
Yes thats right :cold_face: we moved images out of PCS a while back.
If images aren't coming from S3, that could explain the load on PCS.
so it's unrelated to the issue we're having?
Worth checking S3 / CloudFront access.
If S3 is failing, PCS will fallback to serving images directly, which could explain the spike.
why the waf rule is flaring up?
If S3 is unavailable, clients fallback to PCS for images, causing a surge in requests and triggering the WAF rule.
so we need to check the S3
Checking S3 / CloudFront access now.
we may be doing it to ourself with the spike from catalogue
Hamed looking into it
I think I found something.
Image requests at the edge are failing.
ERROR Static asset request failed / status=403 / reason=ExpiredPolicy
what does that mean
CloudFront is rejecting the image requests.
why
Looks like some kind of access policy or signing configuration has expired.
certs? configs?
I'm not sure yet which part of the CloudFront setup is causing it.
When the edge can't serve images, the frontend falls back through ProductCatalogueService.
PCS was never scaled to handle that much traffic.
That explains the traffic spike and the WAF alerts.
Is the business currently losing money due to this internal spike, and what is the ETA for resolution?
It means static image access from S3/CDN is failing, so all image requests are falling back to PCS, causing a traffic spike and triggering the WAF.
Because when direct image fetches from S3/CDN fail with an expiry-style error, the system falls back to PCS, which can't handle the sudden surge in traffic.
It looks like an expired policy or misconfigured signed URL for S3/CloudFront, not a certificate issue.
can we scale PCS up until we resolve?
Alright, thinking through fixes… give me a second.
I see two paths we could take.
Option 1: scale up ProductCatalogueService so it can absorb the fallback image traffic.
we're looking for a solution to mitigate
That would stabilise the platform while images are still failing through CloudFront.
Lets scale up
Option 2: restore the CloudFront access policy so images start loading from S3 again.
then we restore
That fixes the root cause and removes the load from PCS.
mitigation first
Worth discussing before we commit.
Alright, if we go the scale route, here's the plan.
First increase PCS replica count.
Bez if screaming on the other channel
Then raise CPU and memory limits so autoscaling has headroom.
Who's gonna do that?
We'll monitor latency and error rates as traffic shifts.
I'll also adjust the WAF so legitimate traffic isn't blocked.
Scaling PCS replicas now to match frontend request volume.
Alright, let's scale PCS for now. Post updates here.
thanks Maya
Scaling PCS replicas now.
Replica rollout complete.
Increasing CPU and memory limits.
how long to kick in?
Limits updated.
Hamed can you start looking at the config change?
Easing WAF rate limits so legitimate traffic can pass.
pleae
Done.
PCS latency is starting to improve.
Good sign. Let's keep monitoring.
PCS error rates are dropping and request success is trending up as new replicas come online.
I'm handling the PCS scaling and resource adjustments right now.
PCS is stabilising—latency and error rates are nearly back to normal levels.
You're welcome—WAF rules are still tuned for protection, but customer traffic is flowing smoothly now.
PCS scaling changes are already live; full effect should be visible within the next 2-3 minutes as autoscaling settles.
:star: Reviewed changes
Copy that—I'll review the config change now while keeping an eye on PCS stability.
PCS is now stable, error rates are normal, and customer experience has recovered.
still not seeing changesi n the website
It may take another minute for DNS and cache propagation—I'm still monitoring live metrics for improvements.
we've scaled up the catalogue and pressure is easing, waiting for changes to propagate
Let me know as soon as you confirm that sales and revenue are back to normal levels.
we're then looking to update the config
the route through PCS was a fallback as there are config errors on the main S3
we're mitigating and looking to change the config now
still nothing
PCS pods are healthy and serving traffic; if the website isn't updating, it could be a frontend cache or CDN issue—should I flush the CDN cache?
yes pls
I'll check the platform
The images are appearing again for me
Images are visible for me!!
not for me
Latency and error rates are stable, but let's double-check from a few different locations to confirm image delivery everywhere.
How many customers or orders have been affected so far—give me specific numbers.
ok all good for me now. Bob can we check sales?
Customer reports are easing and the images are loading again across the site.
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Images are back. Will get numbers now
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Bob can you get me a number of affected custoemrs? Tinus where are we with the numbers?
Great work everyone, well handled.
Great work everyone
Indeed, for a while I was worried it was a security issue
Turns out it was a false alarm, not something you see every day
I remember Jamie had a similar issue not too long ago

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/38/8350/feedback
Confirm that transactions and revenue are flowing as expected now.
